overview: Golgari Midrange is an efficient, non-blue midrange deck that focuses on maximizing mana usage and acting on its own turns. It relies on a suite of impactful creatures that generate value and apply a steady clock, supported by a strong package of interaction and disruption spells. The deck stands out by avoiding blue and excelling at resource denial and board presence.
win_conditions: Applying a steady clock with efficient creatures like Mosswood Dreadknight and Caustic Bronco
Generating card advantage and value through creature abilities and tutor spells
Finishing the game with Restless Cottage or a wide board of value creatures
Draining opponent's resources with Liliana and Sheoldred

mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking early interaction or threats

examples.reason: Contains early threat, interaction/tutor, and efficient mana sources as described for an ideal starting hand.

early_game: Play interaction to disrupt opponent's resources
Drop early threats to apply pressure
Maximize mana usage on every turn
mid_game: Use tutors like Pillage the Bog and Analyze the Pollen to find lands or threats
Manage board state against opponent's disruption
Apply steady pressure with creatures like Glissa and Trespasser
late_game: Finish the game with Restless Cottage or Liliana's ultimate
Leverage Sheoldred and Dreadknight for massive value
Use removal to clear path for finishers

key_cards.name: Mosswood Dreadknight
key_cards.notes: Creates value through most actions, applies a powerful clock, and synergizes with Sheoldred.

key_cards.name: Caustic Bronco
key_cards.notes: Applies early pressure and generates card advantage.

key_cards.name: Glissa
key_cards.notes: Dodges removal, bypasses defenses, and creates card advantage nearly every turn.

key_cards.name: Restless Cottage
key_cards.notes: Becomes a creature to close games or block attackers; attacking enables extra abilities.

key_cards.name: Sheoldred, the Apocalypse
key_cards.notes: Drains opponent resources and synergizes powerfully with Dreadknight.

key_cards.name: Liliana
key_cards.notes: Drains opponent's resources and sets up a powerful ultimate if stuck on board.

key_cards.name: Pillage the Bog / Analyze the Pollen
key_cards.notes: Fetch important cards from the deck to secure land drops or threats.

sequencing_tips: Save removal for the opponent's bigger, most problematic threats
Attack with Restless Cottage to enable its extra abilities
Play creatures defensively against Boros until you can cast Harvester of Misery
Use Trespasser to remove Memory Deluge from the graveyard in Azorius Control matchups
Be cautious of life-drain abilities in the Boros matchup

matchups.advice: Maximize removal usage and prioritize Sheoldred with Mosswood Dreadknight. Use Tranquil Frillback to gain life.
Navigate heavy disruption and control tools. Use Glissa and Sorin to generate flying threats.
Play defensively until you have enough mana for Harvester of Misery. Deal with main threats and watch for life drain.
Take the aggressor role and attack fast. Use Bronco with Glissa for turn 3 damage and stick Liliana to drain resources.
Maintain an aggressive style and pressure the opponent. Deal with disruptive threats and use creatures effectively against global removal.

overview_evidence.evidence_span: Golgari, as a midrange archetype, is very efficient, but it's still quite different from other midrange lists... it mostly acts on its own turns and uses its mana as much as it can.

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: Mosswood Dreadknight is the heart of this deck. This creature creates value... and puts on a powerful clock on the board

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: try to save your removals for them... attacking is enough to enable them... use your creatures more defensively than offensively.
